title: Quadratics by taking square roots (intro)
description: Solve simple quadratic equations by taking the square root of both sides of the equation.
topic_path: Solving by taking the square root

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$2x^2-5=13$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$3x^2 +4=436$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$6x^2 +1=487$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$-2x^2 -9=-107$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$-x^2 -8=-33$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$5x^2 +3=83$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$x^2 +7=43$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$\begin{align}
x^2 +7&=43
\\\\
x^2&=36
\end{align}$
Now we can take the square root of both sides, not forgetting to take both the positive and negative roots of the right-hand side:

$\begin{align}
\sqrt{x^2}&=\sqrt{36}
\\\\
x&=\pm 6
\end{align}$
In conclusion,

$\begin{align}
\text{lesser }x &= -6
\\\\
\text{greater } x &= 6
\end{align}$
